name: Hamas
description: Designated Foreign Terrorist Organization by the U.S. State Department. Responsible for the October 7, 2023 attack on Israel killing 1,200 people and taking 251 hostages. DSA refused to condemn the attack and framed it as legitimate resistance.

dsa_connections.description: DSA National Convention refused to condemn Hamas October 7 attack
DSA officially endorses BDS movement — co-founded by Hamas-linked figures

name: Hezbollah
description: Iranian-backed Shia militant organization and political party based in Lebanon. Designated Foreign Terrorist Organization. Multiple DSA-endorsed officials have opposed U.S. sanctions on Hezbollah and voted against measures to restrict their financing.

dsa_connections.description: Multiple DSA reps voted against Hezbollah sanctions packages
DSA-aligned figures have appeared at events alongside Hezbollah supporters

name: Council on American-Islamic Relations (CAIR)
description: Named as unindicted co-conspirator in the 2007 Holy Land Foundation Hamas financing trial — the largest terrorism financing prosecution in U.S. history. Major DSA donor network and endorsement partner. Largest Muslim advocacy organization in the United States.

dsa_connections.description: Ilhan Omar headlined multiple CAIR fundraising events

name: BDS Movement
description: Boycott, Divestment, Sanctions movement targeting Israel. Leadership figures have publicly expressed support for Hamas and armed resistance. DSA has been an official BDS endorser since 2017. Co-founded by Omar Barghouti, a documented Hamas supporter.

dsa_connections.description: DSA officially endorsed BDS movement at national convention

name: American Muslims for Palestine (AMP)
description: AMP leadership has been linked by researchers to Holy Land Foundation successor networks. AMP co-sponsors events with DSA chapters nationally and bundles donations for DSA-endorsed candidates. Major Red-Green Axis coordination node.

name: Muslim Student Association (MSA)
description: Founded by Muslim Brotherhood members in 1963. MSA chapters on college campuses serve as a recruitment pipeline for both Islamist political networks and DSA-aligned progressive organizations. Active on 600+ campuses nationwide.

dsa_connections.description: MSA chapters co-host events with campus DSA chapters at multiple universities

name: Justice Democrats
description: Primary electoral vehicle for DSA candidates. Recruits, trains, and funds radical left candidates for federal office. Co-founded by AOC's chief of staff Saikat Chakrabarti. Multiple FEC violations reported. Raised $11.2M in 2022 cycle.

dsa_connections.description: Raised and deployed $11.2M for DSA-aligned candidates in 2022

name: Working Families Party
description: DSA's primary electoral coalition partner. Co-endorses DSA candidates, coordinates ground operations, and shares donor networks. Active in 12+ states with deep ties to SEIU and progressive unions. Raised $6.8M in 2022 cycle.

dsa_connections.description: Co-endorses DSA candidates and coordinates shared voter outreach
